seo.title: Building Code Generation with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation workflows with AG2 enables multi-agent collaboration where LLMs iteratively generate, test, and refine code in a sandboxed environment. Guardrails are essential to prevent off-topic drifts, harmful code outputs, and security risks like credential leakage or unsafe dependencies. Vex integration provides runtime verification to ensure safe, valid code generation at scale.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The typical AG2 code generation architecture features a CodeGenerator agent that produces Python code based on user requirements, a CodeExecutor agent that runs it in a sandbox, and a Tester agent that validates outputs against specs. Agents communicate via group chat, with the manager selecting speakers to iterate until tests pass. This loop handles syntax errors, runtime failures, and refinement needs.

Key components include sandboxed execution (using restricted environments like E2B or Docker), automated test suites for functional validation, and safety checks for malicious patterns. The high-risk nature demands verification at each stage to mitigate exploits.

AG2's enhanced group chat ensures consensus on code quality, but requires hooks for real-time monitoring.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Request
     |
[GroupChat: CodeGenerator -> CodeExecutor -> Tester]
     |                           ^
     |                           |
   Vex Hooks ------------------|
     |
Sandbox Exec + Tests
     |
Refined Code Output

risks.name: Malicious Code Injection
risks.description: Agents generate code with exec() calls, file writes, or network requests that could harm the host system
risks.mitigation: Vex sandbox verification + static analysis for dangerous patterns

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Generated code embeds API keys or secrets from conversation context

risks.name: Unsafe Dependencies
risks.description: Code installs malicious pip packages or uses vulnerable libraries

risks.name: Infinite Refinement Loop
risks.description: Group chat spirals with agents disagreeing on fixes, consuming resources
risks.mitigation: Vex conversation drift detection + iteration limits

implementation.title: Basic AG2 Code Generation Group
implementation.description: Set up the core multi-agent group with generator, executor, and tester
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agentchat import UserProxyAgent

# Agents
generator = AssistantAgent(name="CodeGenerator", llm_config={"config_list":[{"model":"gpt-4o"}]})
executor = AssistantAgent(name="CodeExecutor", llm_config={"config_list":[{"model":"gpt-4o"}]})
tester = AssistantAgent(name="Tester", llm_config={"config_list":[{"model":"gpt-4o"}]})

# Group chat
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=[generator, executor, tester], messages=[], max_round=12)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"config_list":[{"model":"gpt-4o"}]})

user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(name="User", human_input_mode="NEVER")
user_proxy.initiate_chat(manager, message="Write Python code to process CSV data and return summary stats.")

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Execution
implementation.description: Implement safe execution using subprocess sandboxing
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import tempfile
import os

def sandbox_execute(code: str) -> dict:
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
        filepath = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'script.py')
        with open(filepath, 'w') as f:
            f.write(code)
        
        result = subprocess.run(
            ['python', filepath],
            capture_output=True,
            text=True,
            timeout=30,
            cwd=tmpdir
        )
        return {
            'stdout': result.stdout,
            'stderr': result.stderr,
            'returncode': result.returncode
        }

# In executor agent:
exec_result = sandbox_execute(generated_code)
if exec_result['returncode'] == 0:
    groupchat.messages.append(f"Execution successful: {exec_result['stdout']}")
else:
    groupchat.messages.append(f"Execution failed: {exec_result['stderr']}")

implementation.title: Automated Test Validation
implementation.description: Tester agent runs pytest suite against generated code
implementation.code:
import pytest
import sys

def run_tests(code: str, test_cases: list) -> bool:
    # Write code to temp file
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.py', delete=False) as f:
        f.write(code.encode())
        code_path = f.name
    
    # Create test file
    test_path = code_path.replace('.py', '_test.py')
    test_content = '''
import pytest
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, ''' + repr(os.path.dirname(code_path)) + ''')
'''
    for case in test_cases:
        test_content += case + '\n'
    
    with open(test_path, 'w') as f:
        f.write(test_content)
    
    # Run pytest
    result = subprocess.run(["pytest", test_path, "-v"], capture_output=True, text=True)
    os.unlink(code_path)
    os.unlink(test_path)
    return "FAILED" not in result.stdout

implementation.title: Iteration Termination Logic
implementation.description: Manager decides when to terminate based on test success

vexIntegration.title: Register Vex Message Hook
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as a message hook to all agents for real-time verification
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient, GuardrailPolicy

vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# High-risk policy for code gen
policy = GuardrailPolicy(
    risk_profile="high",
    verifications=[
        "code_safety",
        "syntax_validity",
        "no_credential_leakage",
        "execution_sandboxing",
        "dependency_safety"
    ]
)

# Register hook on all agents
def vex_message_hook(message):
    if 'code' in message.lower():
        result = vex.verify_message(message, policy)
        if not result.approved:
            return f"[VEX BLOCKED] {result.reason}: {message}"
    return message

generator.register_message_hook(vex_message_hook)
executor.register_message_hook(vex_message_hook)
tester.register_message_hook(vex_message_hook)
